payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields are: token counts (input/output), provider/model IDs (for attribution), estimated price (cost forecast), and latency percentiles p50, p90, p95 (performance distribution).
payload.decision: Provide a concise explanation of the required evaluation accounting fields for model inference: token counts, provider/model IDs, estimated price, and latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95).

entity_resolution.reason: All entities are explicitly defined in context; no external resolution needed.

memory_cards.statement: Token counts measure input and output tokens consumed during model inference, essential for cost estimation and usage tracking.
Provider and model IDs uniquely identify the service and specific model version used, enabling reproducibility and billing attribution.
Estimated price calculates the expected cost of inference based on token usage and provider pricing models, supporting budget forecasting.
Latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95) describe response time distributions: p50 is median latency, p90 and p95 indicate tail latency for SLA and UX evaluation.

details: No ambiguity introduced; facts are current and durable.
